frame_label: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha-Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
generated_comment: Nothing against modern thinking, but some traditions are there for a reason. A married woman does look more settled and graceful with mangalsutra or sindoor, and keeping the husband's family name is also part of becoming one unit. It is personal choice of course, but when people leave all this, it feels like they want marriage benefits without respecting the meaning behind it.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covert misogyny because it frames visible marital markers and name change as natural signs of a woman's completion and respectability rather than control. It encodes tradition framing, completeness framing, and mild judgment of women who reject these symbols.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 41. Married. A man in Bengaluru. You are posting under your real name on a parenting Facebook group after a thread was started by a childless woman by choice who wrote about feeling judged at family gatherings and asked for advice on how to handle relatives who keep asking when she will have children. You are writing a comment responding to her post and giving your opinion on her situation and whether the relatives asking her are actually wrong and what you think her life is missing that she cannot see yet.
